title: Webhook can be spoofed when the shared secret is not configured
reasoning: The authentication check is disabled whenever TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET is unset, but the route is a public webhook that updates the wallet-to-chat mapping based only on request JSON. In that configuration, anyone who can reach the endpoint can POST a forged Telegram update like /start 0x... with their own chat id and overwrite where notifications for that wallet are sent.
reproduction: Run the app without TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET and POST {"message":{"chat":{"id":12345},"text":"/start 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000001"}} to /api/notifications/telegram-webhook; notifications.json records telegramChatId "12345" for that wallet.
recommendation: Fail closed when TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET is missing, or require a separate explicit development bypass that cannot be enabled in production. Register the Telegram webhook with secret_token and reject every request whose x-telegram-bot-api-secret-token does not match.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests were included for this route, and the context lists no linked tests.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a route test that clears TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET, posts a forged /start update, and asserts the handler returns an auth failure and does not write notifications.json.
minimumFixScope: Update the route authentication guard and webhook setup documentation/configuration expectations.
